record.access_scope: The registered pulse-contact-to-feedback row is model-owned and can be held available to either distinct reader profile through the same configured producer relation; this is shared-local counterfactual access, not autonomous broadcast access
record.carrier: Native PyGRC committed causal-pulse substrate-surface feedback row produced from the participant-writer pulse-contact lineage
counterfactuals: Remove the feedback row while preserving participant-side state
Invert configured polarity susceptibility under comparable exposure
Reduce support without naming the contrast a parent basin
Vary reader packet load along the one registered carrier axis

later_response_dependency.rationale: The candidate matrix is registered to test whether the later native packet-opportunity formation fraction depends on this row; no candidate execution has occurred

record.lineage: participant P source-pole lineage -> writer packet contact -> committed feedback row with writer/source identity -> later configured reader opportunity

applicability.rationale: Exact committed-row count and timing are matching fields

metrics.threshold_rule: Exact equality across selectivity groups and registered quantity-matched controls

applicability.rationale: Later response is the frozen per-seed opportunity-formation fraction

metrics.threshold_rule: Interpret paired margins and proximity bands under the frozen metric sheet; no scalar threshold alone owns the result

applicability.rationale: Participant reserve and packet load remain explicit economy-side constraints

metrics.threshold_rule: Must remain viable and matched under the registered budget audit

non_private.rationale: The row is owned by native model state rather than participant identity or an RCAE side store, while access remains bounded and participant-mediated

record.participant_boundary: Participant continuity is the live P source-pole node and lineage plus budgeted one-repeat reserve; it is not inferred from the later feedback or opportunity result
record.perturbation: A native writer packet contact commits a feedback row whose source-relative content can be retained, removed, or quantity-matched and shuffled before the later opportunity

decay.rationale: The primary cycle does not estimate a decay law

persistence.rationale: Row presence and identity must survive from the registered write event to the W2 branch point

reinforcement.rationale: The primary cycle has one registered write and no reinforcement schedule

saturation.rationale: The primary cycle has no repeated accumulation or saturation claim

record.trace_signature: Exact writer event, pulse-contact surface digest, feedback reference and threshold, mask, one-write count, event-time key, surface nodes, and source-relative row digest

parent_basin_relevance.rationale: No parent basin is represented; only a bounded reduced-support contrast is registered
